table.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; font-size: 11px; font-family: verdana; vertical-align: top;

tr.style: border: 1px #F0F0F0 solid; font-size: 11px; font-family: verdana; vertical-align: top

th.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; padding-top: 3px; padding-bottom: 3px

a.title: The logical name of the element

th.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; padding-top: 3px; padding-bottom: 3px

a.title: Information about the use of the element

th.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; padding-top: 3px; padding-bottom: 3px

a.title: Minimum and Maximum # of times the element can appear in the instance

th.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; padding-top: 3px; padding-bottom: 3px; width: 100px

a.title: Reference to the type of the element

th.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; padding-top: 3px; padding-bottom: 3px

tr.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px; vertical-align: top; background-color: white

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; white-space: nowrap; background-image: url(tbl_bck1.png)

a.title:
Models a ratio of values, i.e. where the numerator and denominator are both pure numbers. The `_valid_proportion_kind_` property of the `PROPORTION_KIND` class is used to control the type attribute to be one of a defined set.
Used for recording titers (e.g. 1:128), concentration ratios, e.g. Na:K (unitary denominator), albumin:creatinine ratio, and percentages, e.g. red cell distirbution width (RDW).
Misuse: Should not be used to represent things like blood pressure which are often written using a '/' character, giving the misleading impression that the item is a ratio, when in fact it is a structured value. Similarly, visual acuity, often written as (e.g.) "6/24" in clinical notes is not a ratio but an ordinal (which includes non-numeric symbols like CF = count fingers etc). Should not be used for formulations.

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

a.style: padding-left: 3px; padding-right: 3px; border: 1px maroon solid; font-weight: bold; color: #301212; background-color: #fdf4f4;; padding-left: 3px; padding-right: 3px; border: 1px maroon solid; font-weight: bold; color: #301212; background-color: #fdf4f4;
a.title: This element has or is affected by constraints ( Type_validity, Precision_validity, Is_integral_validity, Fraction_validity, Unitary_validity, Percent_validity, Valid_denominator )

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

tr.td:
Models a ratio of values, i.
Elements defined in Ancestors:@id, extension, @value, @value
Instances of this logical model are not marked to be the target of a Reference
Constraints: Type_validity, Precision_validity, Is_integral_validity, Fraction_validity, Unitary_validity, Percent_validity, Valid_denominator

tr.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px; vertical-align: top; background-color: #F7F7F7

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; white-space: nowrap; background-image: url(tbl_bck10.png)

vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

tr.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px; vertical-align: top; background-color: #F7F7F7

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; white-space: nowrap; background-image: url(tbl_bck10.png)

a.title: Indicates semantic type of proportion, including percent, unitary etc.

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

tr.td: Indicates semantic type of proportion, including percent, unitary etc.

tr.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px; vertical-align: top; background-color: white

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; white-space: nowrap; background-image: url(tbl_bck10.png)

a.title: Precision to which the `_numerator_` and `_denominator_` values of the proportion are expressed, in terms of number of decimal places. The value 0 implies an integral quantity. The value -1 implies no limit, i.e. any number of decimal places.

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

tr.td: Precision to which the `_numerator_` and `_denominator_` values of the proportion are expressed, in terms of number of decimal places.

tr.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px; vertical-align: top; background-color: #F7F7F7

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; white-space: nowrap; background-image: url(tbl_bck10.png)

vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

tr.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px; vertical-align: top; background-color: white

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; white-space: nowrap; background-image: url(tbl_bck00.png)

a.title: Optional tagged other reference ranges for this value in its particular measurement context.

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

tr.td: Optional tagged other reference ranges for this value in its particular measurement context.

description.value: True if the `_numerator_` and `_denominator_` values are integers, i.e. if `_precision_` is 0.
Sum of two strictly comparable proportions.
Difference between two strictly comparable proportions.
Return True if this `DV_AMOUNT` is considered equal to `_other_`.
True if this Proportion is less than `_other_`. Only valid if `_is_strictly_comparable_to()_` is True.
Return True if the `_type_` of this proportion is the same as the `_type_` of `_other_`.

Models a ratio of values, i.e. where the numerator and denominator are both pure numbers. The `_valid_proportion_kind_` property of the `PROPORTION_KIND` class is used to control the type attribute to be one of a defined set.
Used for recording titers (e.g. 1:128), concentration ratios, e.g. Na:K (unitary denominator), albumin:creatinine ratio, and percentages, e.g. red cell distirbution width (RDW).
Misuse: Should not be used to represent things like blood pressure which are often written using a '/' character, giving the misleading impression that the item is a ratio, when in fact it is a structured value. Similarly, visual acuity, often written as (e.g.) "6/24" in clinical notes is not a ratio but an ordinal (which includes non-numeric symbols like CF = count fingers etc). Should not be used for formulations.

short.value: Models a ratio of values, i.

definition.value:
Models a ratio of values, i.e. where the numerator and denominator are both pure numbers. The `_valid_proportion_kind_` property of the `PROPORTION_KIND` class is used to control the type attribute to be one of a defined set.
Used for recording titers (e.g. 1:128), concentration ratios, e.g. Na:K (unitary denominator), albumin:creatinine ratio, and percentages, e.g. red cell distirbution width (RDW).
Misuse: Should not be used to represent things like blood pressure which are often written using a '/' character, giving the misleading impression that the item is a ratio, when in fact it is a structured value. Similarly, visual acuity, often written as (e.g.) "6/24" in clinical notes is not a ratio but an ordinal (which includes non-numeric symbols like CF = count fingers etc). Should not be used for formulations.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Precision validity must satisfy conditional constraints
Is integral validity must satisfy conditional constraints

expression.value: is_integral() implies (numerator.floor = numerator and denominator.floor = denominator)

human.value: Fraction validity must satisfy conditional constraints

expression.value: (type = pk_fraction or type = pk_integer_fraction) implies is_integral()

human.value: Unitary validity must satisfy conditional constraints
Percent validity must satisfy conditional constraints

definition.value: unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references)

May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the resource. To make the use of extensions safe and managable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

comment.value: There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

short.value: Indicates semantic type of proportion, including percent, unitary etc.

definition.value: Indicates semantic type of proportion, including percent, unitary etc.

short.value: Precision to which the `_numerator_` and `_denominator_` values of the proportion are expressed, in terms of number of decimal places.

definition.value: Precision to which the `_numerator_` and `_denominator_` values of the proportion are expressed, in terms of number of decimal places. The value 0 implies an integral quantity. The value -1 implies no limit, i.e. any number of decimal places.

short.value: Optional tagged other reference ranges for this value in its particular measurement context.

definition.value: Optional tagged other reference ranges for this value in its particular measurement context.

short.value: Models a ratio of values, i.

definition.value:
Models a ratio of values, i.e. where the numerator and denominator are both pure numbers. The `_valid_proportion_kind_` property of the `PROPORTION_KIND` class is used to control the type attribute to be one of a defined set.
Used for recording titers (e.g. 1:128), concentration ratios, e.g. Na:K (unitary denominator), albumin:creatinine ratio, and percentages, e.g. red cell distirbution width (RDW).
Misuse: Should not be used to represent things like blood pressure which are often written using a '/' character, giving the misleading impression that the item is a ratio, when in fact it is a structured value. Similarly, visual acuity, often written as (e.g.) "6/24" in clinical notes is not a ratio but an ordinal (which includes non-numeric symbols like CF = count fingers etc). Should not be used for formulations.

human.value: Precision validity must satisfy conditional constraints
Is integral validity must satisfy conditional constraints

expression.value: is_integral() implies (numerator.floor = numerator and denominator.floor = denominator)

human.value: Fraction validity must satisfy conditional constraints

expression.value: (type = pk_fraction or type = pk_integer_fraction) implies is_integral()

human.value: Unitary validity must satisfy conditional constraints
Percent validity must satisfy conditional constraints

short.value: Indicates semantic type of proportion, including percent, unitary etc.

definition.value: Indicates semantic type of proportion, including percent, unitary etc.

short.value: Precision to which the `_numerator_` and `_denominator_` values of the proportion are expressed, in terms of number of decimal places.

definition.value: Precision to which the `_numerator_` and `_denominator_` values of the proportion are expressed, in terms of number of decimal places. The value 0 implies an integral quantity. The value -1 implies no limit, i.e. any number of decimal places.

short.value: Optional tagged other reference ranges for this value in its particular measurement context.

definition.value: Optional tagged other reference ranges for this value in its particular measurement context.
